headline: Weekly standings — see how every member did in a past tournament week

points.text: a `getWeeklyStandings` [service](#diff-7) guards membership and splits pickers from non-pickers
members who didn't pick still appear (LEFT JOIN), so the week's board is complete

facets.what: A paginated weekly leaderboard for one past tournament, per league
facets.why: The leaderboard only had season totals — there was no per-week view
facets.size: 8 files, ~154 runtime lines (plus a 446-line design doc)

risk.note: additive, read-only query; no schema change; gated by a league-membership check

diagram.title: weeklyStandings request flow
diagram.d2:
shape: sequence_diagram
client: { class: actor }
db: { class: store }
client -> router: weeklyStandings(leagueId, tournamentId)
router -> service: getWeeklyStandings(userId, ...)
service -> service: assert league membership
service -> repo: findWeeklyStandings + countWeeklyStandings
repo -> db: SELECT ... LEFT JOIN pick, result
db -> repo: rows incl. members without picks
repo -> service: rows + total
service -> client: picks + membersWithoutPicks + total
diagram.mermaid:
sequenceDiagram
  client->>router: weeklyStandings(leagueId, tournamentId)
  router->>service: getWeeklyStandings(userId, ...)
  service->>service: assert league membership
  service->>repo: findWeeklyStandings + countWeeklyStandings
  repo->>db: SELECT ... LEFT JOIN pick, result
  db-->>repo: rows incl. members without picks
  repo-->>service: rows + total
  service-->>client: picks + membersWithoutPicks + total

procedures.name: leaderboard.weeklyStandings

title: The core change — query -> shape -> expose
description: The two new Kysely queries, the service that guards and partitions them, and the tRPC procedure that exposes the result.

blocks.title: leaderboard.ts

hunks.header: @@ -113,6 +113,65 @@ export async function findLeaderEarnings(leagueId: string, season: number) {
lines: + * Fetch all members' picks and results for a specific tournament in a league.
+ * Starts from league members (LEFT JOIN picks) so members without picks are included.
export async function countStandings(leagueId: string, season: number) {

blocks.description:
**Two new queries — the weekly data foundation.**

- `findWeeklyStandings` starts from active `leagueMember` and **LEFT JOIN**s `pick` (this tournament, `LOCKED`) -> `golfer` -> `tournamentResult`, so a member who didn't pick still yields a row (null `golferId`)
- ordered by result `earnings` DESC, then `scoreToPar` ASC, then a stable `leagueMember.id` tiebreak; paginated by `limit`/`offset`
- `countWeeklyStandings` counts active members — the pagination denominator

Consumed by [the service](#diff-7).

diagram.title: Why members without a pick still appear

blocks.title: leaderboard.ts

lines: +      message: "You are not a member of this league",

blocks.description:
**`getWeeklyStandings` — guard, fetch, partition.**

- verifies the caller is a league member, else throws `FORBIDDEN`
- runs `findWeeklyStandings` + `countWeeklyStandings` together in `Promise.all`
- splits rows into `picks` (has a golfer; gets a `rank` + numeric coercion) and `membersWithoutPicks` (null golfer)
- returns `{ picks, membersWithoutPicks, total, totalPages }`

Exposed by [the router](#diff-5).

blocks.title: leaderboard.ts
blocks.description:
**New `weeklyStandings` tRPC procedure.**

- `protectedProcedure` query — input `{ leagueId, tournamentId, limit?, offset }` (limit capped at 50)
- threads `ctx.userId` into [the service](#diff-7) for the membership check

title: Docs & roadmap
description: The phase-23 design doc this PR delivers, plus the index/roadmap/agent-memory bookkeeping.

blocks.title: phase-23-weekly-standings-history.md

lines: +**Goal:** Users can cycle through past tournament weeks on the league detail page to see who picked whom and how they did.
+The league detail page currently shows two things:
+- **"This Week" section** — current tournament's picks with live/final results
+There's no way to go back and see what happened in a previous week. Users have to remember (or guess) who picked whom.
+The "This Week" component already renders exactly the right data shape — member name, golfer pick, score, earnings, ranked by earnings. We just need to make it navigable across weeks.

+- **Arrow navigation, not a dropdown.** Left/right arrows on the "This Week" header let users cycle through completed weeks. Keeps the interaction lightweight — no menu to open, scan, and select. Just tap through.
+- **No schema changes.** All data exists: Pick + TournamentResult + Tournament. Weekly standings are computed on-demand by joining these tables for a given league + tournament.

+- **Reuse the existing "This Week" table markup.** The locked picks table (rank, member, pick, score, earnings) is already the right format for past weeks. The only change is the header and data source.

+- **Server-side with search params.** The week is controlled via a `week` search param (`?week=<tournamentId>`). No client-side state needed — arrow links are just `<Link>` elements. This keeps the page SSR and URL-shareable.

+- **Tournament page gets it too.** The tournament page already has active league context from the header (`getActiveLeagueId`). For completed and in-progress tournaments, the weekly standings table replaces the pick banner (`DashboardPickModule`). Upcoming tournaments keep the pick banner as-is.
+- **Current week (default, no `?week` param):** Shows "THIS WEEK" prefix before tournament name. No right arrow. Left arrow navigates to the most recent completed week.
+- **Past week:** Shows tournament name only (no week number — the name is more meaningful). Left arrow goes to the previous week, right arrow goes to the next week (or back to current).
+- **First week of the season:** No left arrow.
+- **Inline picker:** Only shown on current week (when `?week` is absent). Past weeks are read-only.
+Past weeks use the same locked-picks table format:
+- Rank (#), Member, Pick (golfer avatar + name + finish position), Score, Earnings
+- Members without a pick shown as "No pick" with dashes (same as current week)
+New query that fetches all members' picks and results for a specific tournament in a league:
+  // Join: leagueMember → user, pick (LEFT JOIN), tournamentResult (LEFT JOIN)
+  // Filter: league, tournament, ACTIVE members, LOCKED picks
+  // Order by: earnings DESC (nulls last), displayName ASC
+  // Display name resolved via SQL coalesce(display_username, name) — matches existing leaderboard repo pattern
+  // Returns: memberId, userId, displayName, golferId, golferName, golferEspnId,
+This is similar to the existing `findLeagueCurrentWeekPicks` in `pick.ts` but:
+- Accepts any tournament ID (not just the current one)
+- Starts from league members (LEFT JOIN picks) so members without picks are included
+async function getWeeklyStandings(leagueId: string, tournamentId: string, userId: string) {

+Reuse the existing `tournament.schedule` query (already returns all non-excluded tournaments with status, dates, purse, ordered by `startDate`). Filter to COMPLETED/FINALIZING/IN_PROGRESS on the server page to build the `navTournaments` list. No new endpoint needed.
+- [x] `findWeeklyStandings` returns all active members with LEFT JOIN on picks
+- [x] Members without picks appear with null golfer/earnings fields
+- [x] Results are ordered by earnings DESC
+- [x] Membership check prevents non-members from querying
+## PR 2: Frontend — Arrow Navigation on League Detail
+Add `tournament.schedule` to the parallel data fetch. Filter to completed + current tournaments to build the navigation list:
+### 2. Handle `?week` search param + URL builder helper
+const { page, twPage, week: weekParam } = await searchParams;
+// Fall back to most recent completed tournament if there's no current week
+When there's no current tournament (between events), the section defaults to the most recent completed tournament instead of disappearing. The arrow nav is always accessible.
+Add a URL builder helper that threads all active search params through every link on the page:
+function leagueHref(overrides: { page?: number; twPage?: number; week?: string | null } = {}) {
+  const w = "week" in overrides ? overrides.week : weekParam;
+Use `leagueHref()` for all links on the page: pagination, arrow nav, and `twPage` links. Pass `week: null` to explicitly clear the param (e.g., navigating back to current week).
+Arrow nav links always reset `twPage` to 1 so users start at the top of each week's table:
+When `?week` is set and it's not the current tournament, fetch `weeklyStandings` instead of using `currentWeek`:
+From `navTournaments`, find the index of the selected tournament and compute prev/next:
+// If next is the current week tournament, link without ?week param
+Replace the static "THIS WEEK" header with navigable version:
+### 5. Render weekly data for past weeks
+When `weeklyData` is set, render the same table structure as the locked-picks table but sourced from `weeklyData`:
+  ? /* existing "This Week" rendering (planned + locked tables + inline picker) */
+  : /* past week table using weeklyData — same columns: #, Member, Pick, Score, Earnings */
+The inline picker (`LeagueThisWeekPicker`) is only rendered for the current week.
+Thin client component that wraps the week section and detects horizontal swipes:
+  const touchStart = useRef<{ x: number; y: number } | null>(null);
+        // Only trigger if horizontal movement dominates and exceeds threshold
+Wrap the `<section className={styles.liveSection}>` with this component, passing `prevHref` and `nextHref` (same URLs as the arrow links). Arrows remain for desktop and as a visible affordance.
+  /* Tappable arrow buttons — at least 44x44 touch target */
+- [ ] Default view (no `?week`) shows current week when one exists — identical to today's behavior
+- [ ] Default view when no current tournament shows the most recent completed week
+- [ ] Left arrow on current week navigates to the most recent completed tournament
+- [ ] Past week shows weekly picks table with correct data (rank, member, pick, score, earnings)
+- [ ] Members without picks in a past week show "No pick" row
+- [ ] Current user row is highlighted in past weeks
+- [ ] Right arrow from a past week navigates forward; from the week before current, returns to current (no `?week` param)
+- [ ] Left arrow hidden on first week of season
+- [ ] Right arrow hidden on current week
+- [ ] Inline picker only appears on current week
+- [ ] `?week` param is preserved across pagination (`?page=` and `?twPage=`)
+- [ ] Swiping right on mobile navigates to previous week
+- [ ] Swiping left on mobile navigates to next week
+- [ ] Vertical scrolling is not intercepted by swipe detection
+- [ ] Swiping does nothing when there's no prev/next week
+- [ ] Arrow targets are at least 44px on mobile
+- [ ] URLs with `?week=` are shareable — teammate can open the same link and see the same week
+## PR 3: Tournament Page — Weekly Standings for Completed/Active Tournaments

+The tournament page (`/tournament/[id]`) currently shows a `DashboardPickModule` pick banner between the header and the field table for all tournament states. For completed and in-progress tournaments, replace this banner with the league's weekly standings table — who picked whom, their scores, and earnings. Upcoming tournaments keep the pick banner as-is.
+  // NEW: fetch weekly standings for completed/active tournaments
+### 2. Replace pick banner with weekly standings when available
+  <DashboardPickModule ... />  {/* existing pick banner — unchanged for upcoming */}
+Server component that renders the same table format as the league detail weekly view:
+- Rank (#), Member, Pick (golfer avatar + name + finish position), Score, Earnings
+- "est." label on earnings for in-progress tournaments
+- Members without picks shown as "No pick"
+- Links to member season pages and golfer profiles
+This is a presentational component — no data fetching, just renders the props. Reuses the same table structure and CSS patterns as the league detail "This Week" table.
+The tournament page doesn't currently use search params. Add:
+  // Pass limit + offset to weeklyStandings query

+Add styles for the weekly standings table — follow the same patterns as `league-detail.module.css` for the "This Week" table. The table sits in the same slot as the pick banner, between header and field table.
+- [ ] Upcoming tournament: pick banner shows as before (no change)
+- [ ] In-progress tournament: weekly standings table replaces pick banner, shows "est." on earnings
+- [ ] Completed tournament: weekly standings table shows final earnings and positions
+- [ ] Members without picks show "No pick" row
+- [ ] Member names link to season pages, golfer names link to golfer profiles
+- [ ] No active league = no weekly standings table (graceful fallback)
+- **Season standings section** — cumulative totals are untouched
+- **Pick flow** — no changes to pick submission, locking, or planning
+- **Earnings rollup** — no changes to how earnings are calculated
+- **Current week behavior** — default view without `?week` is identical to today
+- **Tournament page pick banner for upcoming tournaments** — still works as-is
+- **~~Week number display~~** — Resolved: just show the tournament name. Users care about the name, not an arbitrary week number. Simpler and avoids index derivation issues.

+- **Weeks with no tournament (off-weeks):** Some weeks may not have a tournament. The arrow navigation naturally skips these since we only include tournaments in the nav list. No special handling needed.
+- **~~"This Week" when no current tournament~~** — Resolved: default to the most recent completed tournament when `currentWeek.tournament` is null. The week section always renders.

blocks.description:
**Phase-23 design doc.**

- the weekly-standings-history plan this PR implements — motivation, query shape, and API contract

blocks.title: roadmap.md

lines: **Goal:** Users can view results for each individual past week, not just cumulative standings.
-- [ ] Add `findWeeklyStandings(leagueId, tournamentId)` repository query — picks + results for a given week
-- [ ] Add tournament selector (dropdown of completed tournaments) on league detail page
+- [x] Add `findWeeklyStandings(leagueId, tournamentId)` repository query — picks + results for a given week
+- [ ] Add arrow navigation on league detail page to cycle through completed weeks
- [ ] Show each member's pick, earnings, finish position, and score for the selected week
- [ ] Weekly rankings for that tournament (who earned most that week)

blocks.description: **Roadmap** — marks phase-23 weekly standings as delivered.

blocks.title: project_doc_structure.md

hunks.header: @@ -12,11 +12,13 @@ Key files and what they cover:
lines: - `roadmap.md` — High-level phased roadmap with checkbox items per phase. Update when phase items complete.
- `ideas/` — Lightweight feature ideas (invite link visibility, commissioner approval, leaderboard virtualization).
- `design-review/2026-04-18/` — Technical audits of specific pages (landing, dashboard, member, planner). Historical snapshots, not living trackers.
+- `plans/active/` — In-progress phase plans (Phase 23 weekly standings, Phase 24 alt payment).
- `plans/completed/` — Phases 0-21, consolidate-pick-ux, desktop-polish, e2e-test-suite all completed. Phase 22 has no separate plan file (tracked only in roadmap).
- `CLAUDE.md` (project root) — brief stack summary, runnable commands, pointer to docs index. Keep commands up to date here.

+**Important:** `docs/INDEX.md` is the actual INDEX file (checked into git). `.personal/docs/INDEX.md` is a symlink or separate copy — always edit `docs/INDEX.md` directly. The Read tool path and the git-tracked path may differ; always verify which file the staged diff references.
**Why:** Knowing which file owns which concept avoids reading all docs on every run.
**How to apply:** Check `CLAUDE.md` for command changes, `tech-stack.md` for stack/tooling changes, active plan for task completion status. Only read what's relevant to the diff.

blocks.description: **Agent-memory bookkeeping** — registers the new phase-23 doc in the doc-updater's structure index.
